channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Apple to pay $95 million to settle lawsuit accusing Siri of eavesdropping
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit accusing the privacy-minded company of deploying its virtual assistant Siri to eavesdrop on people using its iPhone and other trendy devices.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The Future of AI in 2025
item.description: The future of AI in 2025: exclusive predictions from insiders and experts.
item.dc:creator: Max Ufberg, Fast Company

item.title: California begins 2025 with solid start to winter snowpack, but more storms are needed
item.description: California is beginning 2025 with a solid start to the winter snowpack, but more storms are needed to keep the state’s water supplies on track.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: AWS Graviton Adoption on the Rise: Half of All Instances Use Custom Silicon
item.description: AWS's custom silicon, Graviton, is gaining traction with 50% of all instances using it over the past two years. The adoption rate has significant implications for the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Tesla Cybertruck joins short list of EVs eligible for $7,500 tax credit
item.description: The number of EVs and plug-in hybrids currently qualified for a credit is 18 models, down from 22 last year.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Top network and data center events 2025
item.description: Ready to travel to gain hands-on experience with new networking and infrastructure tools? Tech conferences – in person and virtual – give attendees a chance to access product demos, network with peers, earn continuing education credits, and catch a celebrity keynote or live entertainment.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Windsurf Cascade uses AI to help beginners code games and applications
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here. I don’t know how to code.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: Health AI: How Apple can boost public health
item.description: Combined with its existing solutions, Apple’s strategic approach to artificial intelligence (AI) deployment could make a radical difference to public health. Here is how it could achieve that.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Musk defends demonetizing critics' content on X
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk defended his move to demonetize his critics' content on his social platform X and maintained the decision did not violate free speech. "The first amendment is protection for 'free speech', not 'paid speech' ffs," Musk wrote Wednesday on X.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: How a flying ferry could help solve Tahoe’s traffic problem
item.description: "Hydrofoil" technology would transport passengers across Lake Tahoe in a half hour, potentially reducing traffic.
item.dc:creator: Lisa M. Krieger, The Mercury News

item.title: Seis formas de fracasar en la gestión del cambio
item.description: En la era de la transformación digital, el cambio organizativo es constante. Depende de los líderes de TI garantizar que los usuarios absorben y aplican los cambios que sus iniciativas digitales aportan a los flujos de trabajo empresariales.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Tractor Supply’s Rob Mills on the CIO as chief growth officer
item.description: Rob Mills has been instrumental in driving Tractor Supply Co.’s rapid growth, profitability, and world-class customer experience. Mills joined Tractor Supply a decade ago as its CIO, and within four years, his role, influence, and impact had expanded significantly.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Ser CIO en 2025: estos serán los principales desafíos tecnológicos
item.description: Anticipar cuáles serán las tendencias en tecnología para el próximo año tiene un poco de bola de cristal: su propio carácter disruptivo hace que en ocasiones entre en juego una herramienta que hasta entonces no había destacado.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: IRS deploys AI tools to combat emerging tech’s role in new fraud schemes
item.description: AI tools are accelerating the development of deepfake schemes and disinformation campaigns that led to fraud The IRS is trying to keep up. The post IRS deploys AI tools to combat emerging tech’s role in new fraud schemes first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Receiving unexpected international calls? What you need to know
item.description: Beware scam calls: Avoid unknown numbers and protect your personal information. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson talks about the increasingly common scam.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rhode Islanders' data was leaked from a cyberattack on state health benefits website
item.description: PROVIDENCE, R.I. (AP) — Cybercriminals who hacked Rhode Island’s system for health and benefits programs have released files to a site on the dark web, a scenario the state has been preparing for, Gov. Daniel McKee said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Verizon, Nvidia team up for enterprise AI networking
item.description: Verizon and Nvidia have partnered to build AI services for enterprises that run workloads over Verizon’s 5G private network. The new offering, 5G Private Network with Enterprise AI, will run a range of AI applications and workloads over Verizon’s private 5G network with Mobile Edge Compute (MEC).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The future of open source will be messy
item.description: Open Source Initiative (OSI) chief Stefano Maffulli says Meta is “bullying” the industry on the concept of open source. The Economist more colorfully characterizes the dispute as “bare-it-all purists … confronting Meta, the social-media giant controlled by a mankini-clad Mark Zuckerberg.
item.dc:creator: Info World
